message:
fcp-manifest: validate event sections and rate-limit pool config

ProvidesSection::validate iterated over the operations map but did not
iterate over events, so a manifest could declare an event with an
empty description or an explicitly-empty topic string and still pass
validation. Downstream consumers that require a non-empty description
for discovery UIs or a non-empty topic for subscription routing had
to re-validate these fields themselves.

Add EventSection::validate (non-empty description, non-empty topic
when present) and call it per entry from ProvidesSection::validate
with canonical-id enforcement on the event id.

RateLimitsSection::validate previously delegated entirely to
fcp_core::RateLimitDeclarations, which silently coerced unknown unit,
enforcement, and scope strings to defaults. That masked manifest
authoring mistakes like unit = "tokens " (trailing space) or scope =
"instances" (plural typo) as "working as intended" rate limits.

Add RateLimitPoolSection::validate with explicit whitelists:

  - unit   ∈ {requests, tokens, bytes, custom}
  - enforcement ∈ {hard, soft, advisory}
  - scope  ∈ {instance, credential, global}

Unknown strings now raise ManifestError::Invalid with the offending
field path and an expected-values hint, and RateLimitsSection::
validate calls the new per-pool validator before the existing
declarations-level check. The existing
rate_limits_section_unknown_unit_defaults_to_requests test is
renamed to rate_limits_section_unknown_unit_rejected and repurposed
to assert the new rejection behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
